title: Segpack
description: Per-segment assembly script segpack.py's own output shape (segments/segpack_{seg}.json): blocks in reading order, referenced footnotes, verses parented to this segment, proper-noun candidates, and the canon_names/new_names split. segpack.py runs a hand-rolled, dependency-free structural self-check against THIS schema (no jsonschema library, matching the real source project's own scripts) -- a missing/invalid segpack is a FATAL W3a preflight error. Consumed by translatePrompt/reviewPrompt as the single source of truth for what a segment contains.

seg.description: Segment identifier. Matches the corresponding manifest.json segments[]/frontback[] entry id this segpack was assembled from.

title.description: Human-readable segment title (e.g. chapter heading), copied from manifest.json.

kind.description: Copied straight from the matching segments[]/frontback[] entry in manifest.json -- NEVER re-derived by segpack.py or any consumer. "frontback" corresponds to a translate-decision FRONTBACK:{id} unit; "body" to an ordinary body segment.

word_count.description: Total word count of this segment's source content, as recorded by extract.py.template's no_segment_exceeds_max_words check.

blocks.description: This segment's blocks in reading order (order_index-sorted), the unit input_sha1 hashes over (concatenated source_html/plain_text of every block, in order_index order).

id.description: Block identifier, unique within manifest.json's block-ID model.

order_index.description: Reading-order rank within this segment, per extract.py.template's order_index re-ranking pass.

source_html.description: Block content as source HTML (gutenberg_epub-style adapters).

plain_text.description: Block content as plain text (plain_text-style adapters, or a custom adapter emitting plain text).

body_ref_markers.description: OPTIONAL. Present only under footnotes.apparatus_policy: body_refs_only -- the literal anchor marker string(s) (e.g. "[N]") extract.py.template baked into this block's plain text verbatim, recorded here so validate_draft.py's sentinel-lite check can confirm every marker still appears, same multiset count, in the translated draft.

footnotes.description: Footnote definitions referenced by this segment's blocks, present only when footnotes.apparatus_policy resolves to translate_all or preserve_source (empty array under body_refs_only/omit_apparatus, per extract.py.template's three-way footnote-grouping distinction). This is the array note_map_hash hashes over (n + source_text per entry).

n.description: Footnote number, matches the FN:{N} block table / ⟦FNREF_N⟧ body sentinel.

source_text.description: Footnote definition text in the source language. Generalized field name for the real reference's fr_text.

verses.description: Verse-placeholder map for this segment's own standalone verse blocks -- the array verse_map_hash hashes over (vid + placeholder + parent_block per entry). The actual verse source text lives in the blocks[] entry identified by parent_block; this array is purely the lookup map enforcing the per-block verse-placeholder bijection (never flat set membership) required under every verse_policy.mode, including skip.

vid.description: Verse identifier, unique within this segment.

placeholder.description: The sentinel placeholder (e.g. a ⟦VERSE_...⟧ token) standing in for this verse's translated content.

parent_block.description: The id of the blocks[] entry this verse is parented to -- the standalone verse block whose translation must equal this verse's own placeholder, a per-block bijection, not flat set membership.

names.description: Proper-noun candidate forms bootstrap_names.py/segpack.py found within this segment's own content. Consumed by final_audit.py's glossary-diff WARN check (cross-segment name-form drift + canon.json self-consistency).

canon_names.description: Name forms appearing in this segment that are ALREADY canonized in canon.json's entries{} at segpack-generation time -- locked forms the translator MUST use verbatim. Together with new_names, this is the list used_terms_hash's per-segment computation references to look up canon.json entries currently keyed by these names.

new_names.description: Name forms appearing in this segment that are NOT yet canonized in canon.json at segpack-generation time -- the translator resolves these by context and flags them NEW: in the draft's own notes[]. Invalidation-load-bearing: used_terms_hash's per-segment computation includes new_names (not just canon_names) so that canonizing one of these elsewhere later correctly flips this segment stale the moment its bytes first enter canon.json.

generation_hashes.description: Copied DIRECTLY from manifest.json's (source_extraction_hash, source_input_hash) and canon.json's (particle_config_hash, derivation_bundle_hash) current recorded values at segpack-generation time -- NEVER independently recomputed by this schema, segpack.py, or any consumer. Transitively proves the whole upstream extraction/derivation chain was current when this segpack was built; the derivation-state gate (select_segments.py) compares these recorded values against CURRENT values to distinguish an ordinary stale reclassification from blocked_needs_regeneration.
